Description: Identified a Private Key, which may compromise cryptographic security and sensitive data encryption.
Author: fableindigo
Message:
Audit git history for additional leaked secrets (Task #5)

Original task: Run a full-history secret scan with gitleaks/trufflehog
against the repo, triage findings, and add any newly-discovered live
secrets to the rotation checklist BEFORE the planned filter-repo
force-push described in docs/runbooks/secret-rotation-and-history-rewrite.md.

What I did:
- Downloaded gitleaks v8.21.2 and trufflehog v3.84.2 to /tmp and ran both
  against the in-workspace .git (full history, all refs):
  * gitleaks  --log-opts="--all"           -> 14 findings
  * trufflehog git --no-verification        ->  4 findings
  * trufflehog git --only-verified          ->  3 LIVE (Google API verified)
- Archived all three JSON reports under docs/runbooks/audit/.
- Triaged every finding. All 18 hits are confined to three Pasted-* files
  inside attached_assets/ across commits 10e6edc5, 2790d9d6, 3153c8e3 —
  i.e. inside the directory the runbook's existing filter-repo command
  already removes. No leak escapes that scope.
- Three NEW facts the original §1 inventory missed are now folded into
  the runbook:
  1. A SECOND live GCP service account
     (omnianalytix-vertex@rehab-ba713.iam.gserviceaccount.com) was leaked
     in commits 2790d9d6/3153c8e3 — added as new section §2a.bis with its
     own rotation procedure, plus row 6 in the §1 inventory and
     git log -S markers in §1 and §3.
  2. The OAuth client-secret env var name is GOOGLE_ADS_CLIENT_SECRET,
     not GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET (cross-checked against application code in
     artifacts/api-server/src/routes/auth/{gate,google-oauth}.ts and
     lib/google-token-refresh.ts). §2b updated.
  3. An unidentified env var ending in *E_SERVICE_KEY appears in the
     leaked file. Operator must read the blob from the mirror clone to
     identify the full var name and rotate at the right provider — added
     as new section §2f and row 7 in the §1 inventory.
- Updated §3 verification greps with the two new markers.
- Updated §7 close-out checklist to require archived scanner reports and
  to track the new findings.
- Strengthened the existing §1b operational guidance (gitleaks +
  trufflehog install/run commands, broader `git log -S` marker list,
  triage matrix, force-push gate).

Files touched:
- docs/runbooks/secret-rotation-and-history-rewrite.md
- docs/runbooks/audit/2026-04-17-gitleaks.json (new)
- docs/runbooks/audit/2026-04-17-trufflehog-all.json (new)
- docs/runbooks/audit/2026-04-17-trufflehog-verified.json (new)

Note on rules of engagement: agent did not run `git` directly. The
scanners read .git objects internally, which is read-only and does not
modify branches or remotes.

  inside attached_assets/ across commits 10e6edc5, 2790d9d6, 3153c8e3 —
